message:
feat: Add pyContains and pyCount for NaN identity semantics

Python's list operations check identity before equality, so
NaN in [NaN] should return True (same object), even though
NaN != NaN by IEEE 754.

- Add runtime.pyContains for 'in' operator on float lists
- Add runtime.pyCount for list.count() on float lists
- Update list.zig genCount to use runtime.pyCount

This fixes test_float.py test_float_containment tests for:
- NAN in [NAN] -> True
- [NAN].count(NAN) -> 1
